source: PDF source pdftotext extraction confirms correct spelling 'marital' at line 78 of source
evidence: Appears in ¶3, discussing division of marital assets. 'Marital estate' is the legal term for community property; 'martial' is not a legal term. Context and all surrounding usages confirm 'marital' is correct.

The word 'administrate' appears only once in the DB at position 21234 within paragraph 21. The PDF source shows 'administrative' (plural adjective) in the correct context: 'routine evidentiary or administrative issues' (line 403 of PDF text). This is a classic OCR error—the letter 'd' misread as 'te'.

source: Rendered PDF page 4 (p4.png) and verified against DB text
evidence: Rendered PDF clearly shows 'serious difficulty' in the quoted material from the statute. The DB text has 'serous' on line 141, which is a single-character OCR miss (missing 'i'). This appears within a quoted statutory requirement about 'serious difficulty in controlling behavior.'
note: This is a transcription error from OCR - the rendered PDF shows the correct 'serious' spelling. The quote is from statutory language defining 'sexually dangerous individual' and must match the statute exactly.
